hero.title: One tiny watcher. Every layer of your host.
hero.highlight: All of it spoken in fluent OpenTelemetry.
hero.subtitle: HarnessSphere is a single, self-contained binary that watches everything that matters on a machine running the Claw/Harness AI stack — host, container, gateway, the AI harness, its tools and the API calls — and ships it all as clean, standard OpenTelemetry.

stats.layersHint: Host → AI, one pane of glass

why.title: Running an AI agent means running several systems stacked on top of each other.
why.body: A host that can run out of memory. A container that can hit its cgroup limits. A gateway that gets flaky. An AI harness burning tokens and calling tools. And a constant stream of API calls that can start failing.
pain: Five different tools, five dashboards stitched together by hand
Tab-hopping at 3 a.m. trying to find the real cause
No way to tell if the model slowed down because of the gateway or the host
solution: One binary, one pane of glass, one timeline
It lives on the same host, so it correlates an AI slowdown with the exact resource pressure that caused it
Drop it in and start seeing — no runtime to install

layers.title: It models your host as seven isolated layers.
layers.subtitle: Two are Critical — the watcher refuses to run blind without them. The rest are Optional: if they go missing or misbehave, they quietly step aside and never take the watcher down.

host.name: Host
host.desc: CPU, memory, swap, disk and network of the physical or virtual machine underneath everything.

self.name: The Watcher
self.desc: HarnessSphere watches its own back — its footprint and the health of its collection loop.

container.name: Container
container.desc: Reads cgroup v2 stats straight from the kernel. No Docker socket, no runtime API, no extra permissions.

gateway.name: Gateway
gateway.desc: Route latency and connection health, by scraping the gateway's Prometheus endpoint and receiving OTLP.

harness.name: Harness (the AI)
harness.desc: The star of the show. Token usage, durations and AI transactions, following the GenAI semantic conventions.

tools.name: Tools
tools.desc: Every tool the AI invokes, timed and counted, following the GenAI execute_tool span convention.

api.name: API Calls
api.desc: The HTTP and gRPC traffic flowing in and out, correlated with the AI traces above.

resilience.title: A watcher that crashes is worse than no watcher at all.
resilience.subtitle: Resilience isn't a feature here — it's the foundation.

points.title: Three layers of containment
points.body: Every collector runs in its own task. A normal error is caught. An unexpected panic is contained via catch_unwind. A dead task is observed and restarted.

points.title: Critical vs. Optional
points.body: Only Host and Self are Critical. If one fails persistently, the watcher flushes and exits loudly. Everything else degrades, backs off and retries.

points.title: A missing target isn't an error
points.body: No container? No gateway responding? That's Unavailable, not a crash — the collector sits out and keeps probing.

points.title: A dead backend doesn't block collection
points.body: If your OpenTelemetry endpoint disappears, export fails quietly in the background while collection keeps running.

runsAnywhere.title: One binary, statically linked, no runtime to install.
runsAnywhere.subtitle: From a beefy Linux server down to a Raspberry Pi.
runsAnywhere.note: The release profile is tuned for a small, dependency-free binary — optimized for size, LTO, stripped.

ecosystem.title: Designed to fit the real tools people run.
ecosystem.subtitle: These components push their telemetry, so HarnessSphere acts as a local collect-and-enrich hub: it receives what they emit, stamps it with host context, normalizes conventions and forwards one tidy stream.

items.name: OpenClaw
items.desc: Pushes OTLP and exposes Prometheus, emitting standard gen_ai.* metrics and its own openclaw.* keys.

items.name: Hermes Agent
items.desc: Pushes nested OTLP spans for sessions, LLM calls and tools via hermes-otel.

items.name: PicoClaw
items.desc: The ultra-light option — perfect for the Raspberry Pi targets.

cta.title: Start seeing every layer.
cta.subtitle: Build it, point it at your terminal for a first look, then ship to any OpenTelemetry backend.

footer.tagline: The unified OpenTelemetry watcher for the Claw/Harness AI ecosystem.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Configuration and usage manuals for HarnessSphere, from first build to a full OTLP pipeline.
